page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 4149.44 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 116.2 GB/sec and 58.21 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is strong at 304.51 GB/sec, while single-threaded compression and decompression tasks remain average.

The D64as_v5 demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, scoring 26434.76 on PassMark database benchmarks, and processes 63.24 million Redis SET operations per second. It also shows strong performance in LLM inference for small models, processing 3077.17 tokens per second for prompts. Given its high core count and balanced memory density, this server provides qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database hosting, and web serving, though the lack of local storage requires external storage attachments.
description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) operating at 3.525 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. The server does not feature local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier PassMark memory scores, alongside strong database operations and Redis throughput. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average range. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, and small-scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v5 is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual machine with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high-density memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores.
Equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory benchmark scores.
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage included.
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded enterprise applications, and web serving.
